headline: Open-weights coding models and browser-sandboxing techniques broke out this quarter while retrieval-augmented generation lost momentum.
body: Retrieval-augmented generation weakened across the window even as it retained the highest ratio among movers, signaling a rotation away from RAG-centric framing toward long-context and tool-use coding models. New open-weights entrants drove this shift: MiniMax M3 ships million-token context windows with Anthropic and OpenAI endpoint compatibility for coding agents, and GLM 5.2 surfaced as a top-ranked open-source model spanning code generation and 3D work. A cluster of sandboxing and isolation techniques broke out as new entrants, including double iframe, same-origin policy, sandbox, and srcdoc, alongside quantization and hybrid strategy. Agent infrastructure continued to spread into new surfaces, from NVIDIA XR AI for wearables to OpenRouter's Fusion multi-model synthesis and debates over agents managing credentials.
